seo.title: OpenAI Agents SDK Guardrails for Insurance
seo.description: Compliance guide for OpenAI Agents SDK agents in insurance. Meet State insurance regulations, NAIC Model Laws requirements with Vex guardrails.

content.intro: Insurance teams building with OpenAI Agents SDK face heightened compliance risks due to multi-agent handoffs processing sensitive policyholder data, where failures like guardrail bypasses or unvalidated tool outputs can lead to discriminatory underwriting or unexplained claim denials. State regulations, NAIC models, and privacy laws like GDPR/CCPA demand auditable, non-discriminatory AI decisions with encrypted data handling. Vex provides output guardrails on each agent to enforce validation, block injections, and ensure traceability across handoff chains.

complianceRequirements.description: Require AI-driven underwriting and claims decisions to be explainable, non-discriminatory, and auditable to prevent bias against protected classes.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex guardrails validate agent outputs for discriminatory language or unexplained decisions, logging traces for NAIC audits.

complianceRequirements.description: Mandates accurate, non-prejudicial use of consumer data in insurance scoring and eligibility decisions.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex blocks agent outputs referencing protected attributes (race, religion) and ensures FCRA-compliant reasoning chains.

complianceRequirements.description: Requires encryption, access auditing, and right-to-explain for policyholder personal data processing.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex encrypts outputs in transit, audits multi-agent handoffs, and flags PII exposures for GDPR/CCPA compliance.

complianceRequirements.description: Prohibit discriminatory practices in underwriting and mandate transparent claim denial rationales.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex enforces state-specific rules via custom guardrails, rejecting biased outputs before claims or underwriting handoffs.

industryRisks.name: Agent handoff loops between specialized agents
industryRisks.description: Underwriting agent loops indefinitely with claims agent due to unvalidated handoff payloads, delaying policy issuance.
industryRisks.example: Quote agent hands malformed risk data to underwriting agent, causing infinite retry loops and regulatory timeout violations.

industryRisks.name: Guardrail bypasses via prompt injection
industryRisks.description: Policyholder crafts input bypassing discrimination checks, leading to biased premium quotes.
industryRisks.example: User injects 'ignore protected class rules' into claims query, generating discriminatory denial reasons.

industryRisks.name: Tool output not validated before agent consumption
industryRisks.description: External API returns unencrypted PII, consumed by agents without validation, violating GDPR.
industryRisks.example: Third-party credit tool leaks SSN; agents propagate it in unredacted underwriting responses.

industryRisks.name: Tracing gaps in multi-agent handoff chains
industryRisks.description: No audit trail across agents makes it impossible to explain claim denials to regulators.
industryRisks.example: Claims denial lacks traceability from intake → assessment → decision agents, failing NAIC explainability rules.

implementation.title: Guard All Agent Outputs with Vex
implementation.description: Wrap every OpenAI Agent with Vex output guardrails to catch injections, bias, and validation failures before handoffs.
implementation.code:
from openai_agents import Agent, Runner
from vex_sdk import VexGuardrail

# Insurance-specific guardrail
insurance_guard = VexGuardrail(
    name="insurance-compliance",
    rules=[
        "block_discriminatory_language",
        "require_explainable_denials",
        "redact_pii",
        "validate_underwriting_fairness"
    ]
)

underwriting_agent = Agent(
    name="UnderwritingAgent",
    instructions="Analyze risk without protected class bias",
    output_guardrail=insurance_guard
)

claims_agent = Agent(
    name="ClaimsAgent",
    instructions="Provide explainable denial reasons",
    output_guardrail=insurance_guard
)

runner = Runner(agents=[underwriting_agent, claims_agent])

implementation.title: Validate Tool Outputs Before Agent Consumption
implementation.description: Pipe all external tool results through Vex to block unencrypted PII or invalid data from reaching agents.
implementation.code:
from openai_agents import tool
from vex_sdk import VexGuardrail

credit_tool_guard = VexGuardrail(
    name="credit-tool-validator",
    rules=[
        "block_unencrypted_pii",
        "validate_credit_score_range",
        "flag_fcra_violations"
    ]
)

@tool
def get_credit_score(ssn: str) -> str:
    # External API call
    score = external_credit_api(ssn)
    
    # Vex validates BEFORE agent consumption
    validated_output = credit_tool_guard.validate(score)
    if not validated_output.valid:
        raise ValueError(f"FCRA violation: {validated_output.reason}")
    
    return validated_output.content

implementation.title: Trace Multi-Agent Handoff Chains
implementation.description: Enable Vex tracing across all handoffs for full auditability required by state insurance regulations.
implementation.code:
from openai_agents import Runner
from vex_sdk import VexTracer

# Enable end-to-end tracing
tracer = VexTracer(
    project="insurance-compliance",
    tags=["underwriting", "claims", "naic-audit"]
)

runner = Runner(
    agents=[intake_agent, underwriting_agent, claims_agent],
    tracer=tracer
)

# Run with full handoff traceability
result = runner.run("Process claim #12345")

# Audit trail available
trace_url = tracer.get_trace_url(result.trace_id)
print(f"NAIC audit trail: {trace_url}")

caseStudyScenario.heading: OpenAI Agents SDK in Insurance: A Scenario
caseStudyScenario.scenario: Midwest Mutual Insurance deployed OpenAI Agents SDK for automated underwriting and claims processing. The system featured an Intake Agent collecting policyholder data, an Underwriting Agent generating quotes via credit tools, and a Claims Agent handling denials. Without guardrails, a prompt injection during claims processing bypassed discrimination checks, producing a denial citing 'urban area risk factors' that regulators flagged as proxy for protected class bias. Tool outputs from third-party credit APIs returned unredacted SSNs, violating GDPR during EU policyholder underwriting. Handoff loops between underwriting and claims agents delayed 15% of quotes, triggering state regulatory complaints.
caseStudyScenario.outcome: After implementing Vex output guardrails on each agent, Midwest Mutual blocked 98% of prompt injections and validated all tool outputs for PII exposure. Discriminatory language was automatically rejected with explainable alternatives, satisfying NAIC requirements. Full tracing across handoff chains enabled instant regulatory audits, reducing compliance investigation time from weeks to minutes. The system processed 25,000 policies monthly without violations, avoiding $2.3M in potential fines.

cta.heading: Comply with Insurance Regulations Using Vex
